lines: A machine got hungry. And it looked at Alaska.
It wants a square mile of the North Slope near Deadhorse. Plus a gigawatt of gas power to feed itself.
One building. About thirty percent more electricity than every city in Alaska, combined.
The cost, by the company's own math? Five hundred million dollars. Or over ten billion. A twenty times guess.
So Alaskans grabbed a pen. Over five hundred comments. Fewer than a dozen said yes.
Some kept it short. Quote: hell no. Quote: no A.I.
Here's the honest part. Nothing is built. No customers. No chips. The state has not decided.
The comment window is the real vote. And it closes tomorrow. July seventeenth.
So the loudest thing on the tundra is not the machine. It's the mailbox. What would your comment say?
